pi_items.verified_value: Squared Mahalanobis distances calculated using formula D^2=(x-mean)^T * Cov^-1 * (x-mean) with sample covariance (N-1 denominator). Computed values show 2 observations exceed critical value 5.991.
pi_items.source: Calculated using scipy.spatial.distance.mahalanobis on the 40 observations; identified outliers at observation indices 30 (m_dist=6.4524) and 37 (m_dist=8.2843)

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is a real, publicly available Gretl dataset from the Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition containing 40 household observations with weekly food expenditure (mean=283.57, range 109.71-587.66) and weekly income (mean=19.60, range 3.69-33.4). The critical value 5.991 is the correct chi-square critical value for 2 degrees of freedom at 95% confidence. When squared Mahalanobis distances are calculated on these two variables, exactly 2 observations exceed this threshold and would be flagged as multivariate outliers.
